text.title: AI Convention Anchoring Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding AI Convention Anchoring. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Repo has exactly one CONVENTIONS.md as policy source; per-dir AGENTS.md references it.
rule.rationale: Repo has exactly one CONVENTIONS.md as policy source; per-dir AGENTS.md references it.

rule.statement: Agents load AGENTS.md (local) before CONVENTIONS.md (global); narrower context wins on conflict.
rule.rationale: Agents load AGENTS.md (local) before CONVENTIONS.md (global); narrower context wins on conflict.

rule.statement: Every recurring violation gets a lint rule; chat-only enforcement is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Every recurring violation gets a lint rule; chat-only enforcement is forbidden.

rule.statement: Lint runs in pre-commit; failed lint blocks the commit.
rule.rationale: Lint runs in pre-commit; failed lint blocks the commit.

rule.statement: Reviewer style comments must be backed by a lint rule or escalate to CONVENTIONS.md update.
rule.rationale: Reviewer style comments must be backed by a lint rule or escalate to CONVENTIONS.md update.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ai-convention-anchoring.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
